evidence_samples.claim: Microsoft will release its next earnings report on Apr 28, 2026.
Last quarter, Microsoft reported EPS of $3.903 versus $4.14 expected by the market.
Microsoft has a Fiscal Year 2025 First Quarter Earnings Conference Call listed on its investor relations events page.
A Microsoft Fiscal Year 2025 First Quarter Earnings Conference Call event is listed for September 10, 2024 at 12:25 PM PT.
The world of mergers and acquisitions within the Microsoft partner ecosystem is evolving rapidly.
In the latest episode of 'Profiles and Partnership,' hosts Anthony Carrano and Rudy Rodriguez sit down with Tim Mueller, managing partner and co-founder of IT ExchangeNet.
Successful acquirers focus teams on the value of the deal, use cross-functional teams to manage interdependencies, and ensure clear alignment on integration priorities and strategy.
Forrest Advisors presents Microsoft, Amazon, Disney, AB InBev, and Verizon as M&A case studies.
The author of the LinkedIn article completed several acquisition integration assignments for Microsoft over the course of five years.
In its 2025 Annual Report, Microsoft states that proceeds from debt issuances were or will be used for general corporate purposes, which may include working capital, capital expenditures, repurchases
MSFT trades at a premium to most peers.
A Microsoft peer comparison can be done using metrics such as operating margin, free cash flow yield, and forward earnings multiples.
Microsoft's current key valuation ratios include PE, PB, PCF, and PS.
Microsoft's valuation ratios are compared to the Software & Programming Industry and the Technology Sector.
Microsoft stock valuation can be compared side by side with industry peers and the market using key valuation metrics.
MarketChameleon provides side-by-side comparison of Microsoft versus peers on income and expenses, growth rates, profitability, stock performance, and valuation ratios.
Seeking Alpha compares Microsoft with other stocks using metrics including valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, EPS revisions, dividends, and ratings.
OnePager provides a sample timeline of FDA approvals for a drug company.
The OnePager timeline shows past and predicted approval dates for each new drug development project.
FDA Tracker's calendar integrates PDUFA dates, clinical trial primary completion dates, and working capital runway estimates into a single timeline.
FDA Tracker's timeline covers U.S.-publicly traded companies facing upcoming PDUFA dates.
The FDA approved 50 new drugs in 2024.
In 2024, 48% of newly approved FDA drugs were first-in-class.
In 2024, 52% of newly approved FDA drugs targeted rare diseases.
As of March 6, 2026, Microsoft Corp has a DCF-derived fair value of $437.42 per share.
Microsoft Corp's current market price was $410.68 as of March 6, 2026.
Microsoft Corp's DCF valuation implied a potential upside of 6.5% as of March 6, 2026.
Microsoft's market share relative to its competitors was reported for Q4 2025 and for the trailing 12 months, based on total revenues.
As of March 2026, Microsoft had a market capitalization of $2.770 trillion USD and was the world's 4th most valuable company.
Windows 10 reaches end-of-support on October 14, 2025.
Melius Research lowered its price target on Microsoft to $400 and maintained a Hold rating.
TipRanks reports that Microsoft has 23 disclosed risk factors, with Tech & Innovation identified as the top risk category.
Microsoft was down 20% year-to-date as of March 24, 2026.
Microsoft Investor Relations has a balance sheet page for FY25 Q4.
Microsoft states that information on the FY25 Q4 balance sheet page is unaudited unless otherwise noted or accompanied by an audit opinion.
MacroTrends provides Microsoft balance sheet data covering 2011-2026.
MacroTrends says it has ten years of annual and quarterly balance sheets for Microsoft.
MarketBeat labels a Microsoft financials page as 2026 financials.
A 2026 competitor list identifies 15 Microsoft competitors and alternatives.
A 2026 competitor list identifies 12 Microsoft competitors and alternatives.
In 2025, Microsoft's cloud computing division became a focal point for antitrust investigations under the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.
Microsoft was involved in a legal dispute over a $50 billion OpenAI-Amazon cloud deal.
Microsoft stock fell 1.61% amid legal and regulatory pressure tied to Azure/OpenAI-Amazon cloud disputes.
As of 2025, the U.S. Federal Trade Commission continued its investigation into Microsoft.
Microsoft makes money through three core business segments.
Microsoft's Productivity and Business Processes segment represents about one-third of the company's revenue.
In a 2026 company profile, Microsoft is described as being organized into three equally sized broad segments.
In FY2025, Microsoft's biggest revenue segment was Server Products and Cloud Services, representing 23.93% of total revenue.
In FY2025, Microsoft's smallest revenue segment was Other Products and Services, representing 0.02% of total revenue.
Microsoft reported segment revenue and operating income for FY25 Q4.
Microsoft recast certain prior period amounts in its FY25 Q4 segment reporting.
Statista's Microsoft segment revenue dataset covers fiscal years 2012 through 2025.
Microsoft's revenue is divided into three main business segments.
MarketScreener provides a ten-year segmentation history for Microsoft.
Microsoft outlined a Q4 2025 target of about $32–$35 billion in Productivity and Business Processes revenue.
The average price target for Microsoft (MSFT) is $594.84, based on 46 reports.
A federal case against Microsoft Corporation and others was filed on March 17, 2026 in the Illinois Northern District Court.
Microsoft is fighting a $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences.
In the UK cloud licensing case, Microsoft was accused of overcharging thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software on cloud services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba.
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's pay package jumped to $96.5 million in fiscal 2025.
Satya Nadella's total compensation was $96.4 million in fiscal year 2025, up nearly 22% from $79.1 million the previous year.
In FY2025, Satya Nadella's stock awards were $84.2 million, cash incentive was $9.55 million, and base salary stayed at $2.5 million.
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's compensation reached about $96.5 million in fiscal year 2025.
Satya Nadella received a 22% pay rise, with compensation hitting $96.5 million in fiscal 2025.
Microsoft's three-year Total Shareholder Return ranked at or above the 80th percentile relative to the S&P 500.
MarketBeat lists Microsoft's current analyst price target at $591.87.
Zacks says 46 analysts have a short-term average price target of $594.84 for Microsoft.
According to Zacks, Microsoft's analyst price targets range from $392.00 to $678.00.
TipRanks says Microsoft's stock forecast is based on 36 analysts offering 12-month price targets in the last 3 months.
Public.com reports a Wall Street analyst price target of $595.27 for Microsoft.
Public.com says its Microsoft price target implies a 0.00% increase from the current stock price.
Tech I cites Microsoft Q2 FY2026 revenue of $81.3B.
Tech I says Azure grew 39% in Q2 FY2026.
Tech I reports Microsoft had a $625B RPO backlog in Q2 FY2026.
Tech I says Microsoft analyst price targets go up to $655.
In FY2025, Microsoft's revenue breakdown by region showed the United States as the largest region at 51.31% of revenue, while Non-US accounted for 48.69%.
In a 2025 CRM comparison, Dynamics 365 Sales and Salesforce Sales Cloud were scored on 87 CRM features across 12 feature groups.

Through the 90-day period concluding February 19, 2026, Microsoft's top three insider stakeholders were CEO Satya Nadella with 786.88K shares, EVP & CFO Amy Hood with 567.57K shares, and Vice Chair an
Microsoft's total shares are held approximately 70.51% by institutional investors and 0.04% by company insiders.
In 2025, Vanguard collectively held more than 6% of Microsoft's total equity.
In Q4 FY2025, Microsoft's Intelligent Cloud revenue was $29.9 billion, up 26% year over year.
In Q4 FY2025, server products and cloud services revenue increased 27%, driven by 39% growth in Azure and other cloud services revenue.
For fiscal 2025, Azure and other cloud services revenue exceeded $75 billion, up 34% from the prior year.
In fiscal 2025, Microsoft Cloud annual revenue increased 23%.
In Q3 FY2025, Intelligent Cloud revenue was $26.8 billion, up 21% year over year.
In Q3 FY2025, Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 33%, with AI services contributing 16 percentage points of that growth.
Microsoft filed its Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025 on 07/30/2025.
Microsoft's 2025 Annual Report says its Responsible AI Transparency Report was in its second annual edition.
An ITPro report said Microsoft had invested nearly $14 billion into an AI startup partnership under FTC scrutiny.
Microsoft repurchased $18.42 billion of common stock in FY2025.
Microsoft paid $24.08 billion in dividends in FY2025.
Microsoft spent $64.55 billion on property, plant and equipment in FY2025.
Microsoft's capital expenditure increased to $64.55 billion in FY2025 from $44.48 billion in the prior year, an increase of about 45.1%.
Azure cloud revenue grew 33%, with AI contributing 16 percentage points to Azure growth.
Microsoft reported $70.1 billion in revenue and earnings per share of $3.46.
A pricing model for Microsoft 365 that uses tiered bundling, per-user licensing, and feature segmentation can increase average contract value by 40-60% versus à la carte pricing.
US Cloud's analysis says Microsoft's 2025-2026 price changes would impose a mandatory 25% cost increase on a typical enterprise agreement profile.
The representative enterprise profile used in US Cloud's Microsoft pricing analysis is a $10 million annual Enterprise Agreement.
Microsoft filed its Form 10-K annual report for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025, on July 30, 2025.
Microsoft invested $64.6 billion in property and equipment during fiscal year 2025.
Microsoft reported revenue of $281.7 billion for fiscal year 2025, up 15%.
Microsoft reported operating income of $128.5 billion for fiscal year 2025, up 17%.
Microsoft Cloud gross margin was 69% in FY25 Q4.
Operating expenses increased by $3.8 billion, or 6%, in FY25 Q4.
Microsoft said it would continue investing in capital expenditures to support growth in its cloud offerings and AI infrastructure and training.
Microsoft estimates the total addressable market for partners at $777 billion in SME by FY26.
Microsoft estimates the total addressable market for partners at $592 billion in Enterprise by FY26.
Microsoft's Q4 FY2025 results included 39% Azure growth.
Microsoft estimates, based on IDC data from October 2024, that the total addressable market for SMC in FY25 is $661 billion.
Of Microsoft's estimated $661 billion SMC TAM in FY25, $467 billion is in cloud solutions.
Microsoft says the SMC TAM is growing at a 15% compound annual growth rate over the next 4 years.
Microsoft says cloud within the SMC TAM is growing at a 20% compound annual growth rate over the next 4 years.
Microsoft's SMC TAM figures apply to companies with fewer than 3,000 employees.
In Microsoft FY2025 Q3, Microsoft Cloud revenue was $42.4 billion, growing 20% year over year and 22% in constant currency.
In Microsoft FY2025 Q3, Microsoft Cloud gross margin was 69%, in line with expectations, and down 3 percentage points year over year due to scaling AI infrastructure.
In Microsoft FY2026 Q1, capital expenditures were $34.9 billion and were described as far exceeding forecasts.
In Microsoft FY2026 Q2, cloud gross margin was 67%, the lowest in approximately three years, with a 400-500 basis point year-over-year contraction.
In Microsoft FY2026 Q2, gross margin came in just over 68%, the narrowest level in three years.
In Microsoft FY2026 Q2, Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 39% year over year.
In Microsoft FY2025 Q4, company gross margin was 69%, down 1 percentage point year over year.

report_sections.title: MSFT — Long, $435.00 Price Target, 33/100 Conviction
report_sections.preview: MICROSOFT CORPORATION — Long at 33/100 conviction. We're Long at 33/100 conviction; fair value about $435 (+17.2% vs spot).

report_sections.title: Variant Perception & Thesis
report_sections.preview: We rate MSFT a Long with conviction 33/100 based on a rare combination of 14.9% FY2025 revenue growth, 45.6% operating margin, and a balance sheet that remains lightly levered even as capex has stepped up sharply. Our variant view is that the market

report_sections.title: Key Value Driver: cloud and AI demand converting into high-return infrastructure spend
report_sections.preview: For Microsoft, the single most important valuation driver is not generic software growth; it is whether rising cloud and AI demand is monetized fast enough to justify a much heavier infrastructure buildout. The source snapshot shows unusual coexistence of strong growth, elite margins, and surging ca
